 {"id":"bd-7l9.1","title":"Initialize Cargo workspace with crate skeleton","description":"Create the Cargo.toml workspace root and empty crate directories for the modular FrankenMermaid architecture. Crate structure: fm-core (core types/IR/config/errors), fm-parser (mermaid syntax parser), fm-layout (layout algorithms), fm-render-svg (SVG output), fm-render-canvas (Canvas2D/WebGPU for browser), fm-render-term (terminal renderer), fm-wasm (WASM bindings), fm-cli (CLI binary). Use Rust 2024 edition, nightly toolchain. Release profile: opt-level=z, LTO, codegen-units=1, panic=abort, strip=true. The fm-layout crate gets opt-level=3 for compute-heavy inner loops. Each crate gets a Cargo.toml with appropriate dependencies on sibling crates and a src/lib.rs (or src/main.rs for fm-cli) stub.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:00.353017579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:07:48.661806905Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T01:07:48.661784724Z","close_reason":"Completed workspace skeleton and validation gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-7l9.2","title":"Configure rust-toolchain.toml and CI/CD pipeline","description":"Create rust-toolchain.toml specifying nightly channel. (2) wasm-build job: install wasm-pack, build fm-wasm for wasm32-unknown-unknown. (3) coverage job with cargo-llvm-cov. Add .gitignore for target/, pkg/, *.wasm, node_modules/.  {"id":"bd-7xyz","title":"Update README FNX section to honestly reflect current integration status","description":"## Problem
README Section "Optional FNX Integration" describes FNX (franken_networkx) as if it's a simple optional feature flag you can toggle on. In reality:
- There are ~25 open P1 beads under the bd-ml2r epic covering architecture, adapter, diagnostics, cycle scoring, ordering enrichment, telemetry, runtime budgets, UX controls, comprehensive verification, and rollout.
- Only 2 of those 25 beads are in-progress (bd-ml2r.1 and bd-ml2r.1.1 — the very first sub-tasks).
- The feature flags exist in Cargo.toml but the actual integration code is not yet written.

This is the project's most ambitious unfinished initiative, and the README makes it sound like a toggle.

## Fix
Update the README FNX section to:
1. Keep the existing Cargo feature flag documentation (it's accurate for the BUILD story).
2. Add a clear status note: "FNX integration is currently in early development. The feature flags compile but the integration surface is not yet functional. See the project's issue tracker for the bd-ml2r epic tracking this work."
3. Optionally add a brief roadmap: Phase 1 (undirected graph intelligence + diagnostics) → Phase 2 (directed graph analysis + ordering enrichment).

## Why This Matters
Understating the gap between "feature flag exists" and "feature works" is a soft form of README dishonesty. Users or contributors who enable `--features fnx-integration` and find it does nothing will lose trust. Honest disclosure costs nothing and builds credibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
- README FNX section includes honest status disclosure.
- No false impression that enabling the feature flag gives you working functionality.
- Roadmap or link to issue tracker for follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:48.482538455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:27:44.517402065Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:27:44.517103149Z","close_reason":"Added honest status disclosure (early development, feature flags compile but not functional), roadmap (Phase 1/2), and link to FNX_INTEGRATION.md ADR.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7xyz","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:04.465716866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-keyp","title":"Flowchart: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# Flowchart Feature Parity

## Current State
Flowchart is the most complete diagram type (~90% parser, ~85% SVG render). The core graph structure (nodes, edges, subgraphs, directions) works well. The gaps are in styling directives, interactive features, and constraint hints.

## Gaps to Close
1. classDef directive: define reusable style classes
2. style directive: apply inline styles to specific nodes
3. linkStyle directive: style specific edges
4. click directive rendering: make nodes clickable with URLs/callbacks
5. Tooltip support on hover
6. accDescription accessibility descriptions
7. Subgraph direction override (direction within subgraph)
8. Node markdown label support (using backtick syntax)

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/flowchart.html
- Parser: crates/fm-parser/src/mermaid_parser.rs lines 777-986
- SVG renderer: crates/fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:42:02.971032563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:19:41.813234263Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:19:41.813007033Z","close_reason":"All 8 feature gaps are now closed: classDef (bd-bfsm), style/linkStyle (bd-y6o8), click+tooltip (bd-peow), subgraph direction (bd-xwm3), markdown labels (bd-7ib6), parallel edge syntax (bd-bium). accDescription was already implemented via extract_accessibility_directives.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-7ib6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:40.904059282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-bfsm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:37.663093628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-bium","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:30.829700237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-peow","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:39.308730074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-xwm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:40.108139881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-y6o8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:38.499539334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-kugr","title":"Restore build-wasm.sh wasm size budget after fm-wasm package regeneration","description":"bd-b0pc.3 fixed the npm package branding and the wasm32 build blockers, but rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_fm_wasm ./build-wasm.sh still exits nonzero because the generated pkg/frankenmermaid_bg.wasm is over the enforced gzip budget.

Current evidence:
- build now completes wasm-pack + wasm-opt and regenerates branded npm artifacts successfully
- final size gate reports raw wasm 1520341 bytes and gzipped wasm 602381 bytes
- budget is 512000 bytes, so the run fails only at the post-build size check

Scope:
1. Determine which recent fm-wasm/fm-layout changes account for the oversized browser bundle
2. Reduce the wasm bundle back under the existing 500 KiB gzip budget or intentionally revise the budget with evidence
3. Keep the branded npm package metadata and README regeneration added in bd-b0pc.3 intact
4. Verify ./build-wasm.sh exits 0 again via rch exec -- ...

Cross-reference:
- surfaced while finishing bd-b0pc.3
- blocks a fully clean wasm packaging verification run","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T23:39:46.941741509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:30:00.867518105Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:30:00.867206226Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","packaging","performance","wasm"]}
 {"id":"bd-ma11","title":"EPIC: Eliminate Hardcoded Constants and Make Rendering Theme-Aware","description":"# EPIC: Eliminate Hardcoded Constants and Make Rendering Theme-Aware

## Background
A comprehensive audit across all sessions identified two categories of hardcoded values that undermine correctness:

1. **Hardcoded colors in renderers** that bypass the theme system, causing poor/invisible output on dark, high-contrast, or custom themes. These are functional bugs — the output is wrong for ~60% of theme presets.

2. **Hardcoded layout dimensions** (chart sizes, margins, spacing) that should be configurable or computed from content. These produce acceptable output for typical diagrams but fail to adapt to unusual content (very long labels, many data points, small terminals).

## Why This Matters
frankenmermaid ships with 10 theme presets (Default, Dark, Forest, Neutral, Corporate, Neon, Pastel, HighContrast, Monochrome, Blueprint). Any hardcoded color that works on the Default theme but fails on Dark/Neon/HighContrast is a bug that affects every user who switches themes. The theme system exists specifically to avoid this — the hardcoded values are bypassing it.

For layout constants, the issue is less acute but still important: pie charts with 20+ slices, quadrant charts with long axis labels, and gantt charts with many sections all need their layout to adapt rather than using fixed 400px dimensions.

## Scope
- **In scope**: All hardcoded hex colors, all hardcoded chart dimensions, all hardcoded spacing that should come from LayoutSpacing or config
- **Out of scope**: Deliberate visual design constants (rounded corners, dash patterns, opacity values, font size multipliers) that are independent of theme/content

## Success Criteria
- Zero hardcoded hex color strings in SVG/Canvas renderers outside of theme.rs and tests
- All chart layout dimensions derived from config or content metrics
- All 10 theme presets produce readable, visually correct output for every diagram type
- No regression in golden snapshot tests (bless after fixes)

## Architecture Note
The fix strategy uses three approaches depending on context:
1. **Direct theme reference** (preferred): Pass `&theme.colors.X` where theme is in scope
2. **CSS custom properties** (for functions without theme param): Use `var(--fm-text-color, #fallback)` which the embedded theme CSS defines
3. **Config extension** (for layout dimensions): Add fields to LayoutSpacing or create diagram-specific config structs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:23.946145931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:10.864041670Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:10.863558044Z","close_reason":"All 12 subtasks completed: 6 hardcoded color fixes (SVG notes, canvas notes, band/axis labels, cluster dividers, diagram title, quadrant fills), 4 layout constant extractions (sequence spacing, pie/quadrant adaptive sizing, legend config), 1 canvas color audit, 1 theme regression test  {"id":"bd-mcva","title":"Clean up parser test garbage files at repo root","description":"## Problem
Parser tests or malformed input handling occasionally creates garbage files at the repo root:
- B[🚀 (zero-byte mojibake file, created 2026-03-31)
- Potentially other B* files matching the parser test output pattern

These are artifacts of malformed diagram parsing that leak to the filesystem. The .gitignore now covers B[* and B*nend* patterns, so they won't be committed, but the files remain on disk.

## Scope
1. Delete the B[🚀 file (requires explicit user permission per AGENTS.md Rule 1).
2. Investigate WHERE in the parser these files are created. If the parser is writing to the filesystem during normal operation, that's a bug — parsers should never create files as a side effect of parsing.
3. If it's a test artifact, ensure the test cleans up after itself.

## Root Cause Investigation
The file appears to be created when a Mermaid input string starting with "B[" is interpreted as a file path by the CLI's input handling (stdin vs file detection). The CLI likely tries to open "B[🚀" as a file when it should be treating it as literal diagram text. This could be a real bug in input routing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Garbage file deleted.
- Root cause identified and documented (is it a CLI input routing bug or a test artifact?).
- If CLI bug: fix the input path detection to not create spurious files.
- If test artifact: test cleanup added.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:03.218997278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:06.871880999Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mcva","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:06.871321790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-tl00","title":"Replace hardcoded sequence note colors with theme-aware values","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded yellow/orange hex colors for sequence diagram notes in both SVG and Canvas renderers with theme-derived values.

## Why
Sequence notes currently render with hardcoded colors:
- SVG: `fill="#fffde7"` (light yellow), `stroke="#f9a825"` (orange) — lines ~1641-1642 in fm-render-svg
- Canvas: `fill="rgba(254,249,195,0.9)"`, `stroke="#ca8a04"`, text `fill="#713f12"` — lines ~833-841 in fm-render-canvas

These are invisible or jarring on Dark, Blueprint, Neon, and Monochrome themes.

## Technical Context
**SVG renderer**: The `theme` variable IS in scope where notes are rendered (inside `render_svg_with_layout`). We can directly use `theme.colors.node_fill` for background and a theme accent for border.

**Canvas renderer**: The `self.config` struct has `node_fill`, `node_stroke`, `label_color` fields. These should be used instead of hardcoded values.

## Specific Changes
**SVG** (fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs):
- Note background: Change to `theme.colors.node_fill`
- Note border: Change to a theme accent color (e.g., `theme.colors.accents[4]`)
- Note text: Already uses `theme.colors.text` — correct

**Canvas** (fm-render-canvas/src/renderer.rs):
- Note background: Change to `self.config.node_fill`
- Note border: Change to `self.config.node_stroke`
- Note text: Change to `self.config.label_color`

## Status
SVG DONE (latest session). Canvas still has hardcoded colors at lines ~833-841.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Notes are readable on all 10 theme presets
- No hardcoded hex colors for notes in either renderer
- Note colors harmonize with the rest of the diagram","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:56.629193444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:32:30.353999551Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:32:30.353387673Z","close_reason":"Canvas note colors now use self.config.node_fill/node_stroke/label_color. SVG was fixed in prior session.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-tvxc","title":"Class: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# Class Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Class diagrams parse basic class declarations and relationships (~75% parser). The main gaps are member visibility modifiers, type annotations, generics, stereotypes, and namespace grouping.

## Gaps
1. Visibility modifiers (+, -, #, ~) on members/methods
2. Type annotations (returnType, parameterTypes)
3. Generic/template parameters (Class~T~)
4. Stereotypes (<<interface>>, <<abstract>>, <<enum>>)
5. Annotations and notes
6. Namespace grouping
7. Cardinality/multiplicity on relationship ends
8. Direction specification (class diagram direction)

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/classDiagram.html

 {"id":"bd-u4c1","title":"Replace hardcoded SVG band/axis colors with CSS custom properties","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded hex color strings in `render_layout_band()` and `render_layout_axis_tick()` functions in fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs with CSS custom property references.

## Why
These functions render gantt section labels (#4a5568), axis tick marks (#94a3b8), and axis tick labels (#64748b). These colors are theme-unaware — they are legible on the Default light theme but invisible on Dark, Neon, and Blueprint themes where the background is dark.

## Technical Context
These two functions (`render_layout_band` at line ~2050 and `render_layout_axis_tick` at line ~2104) only receive `&SvgRenderConfig` — they do NOT have access to the `Theme` struct. The options are:
1. Thread `&ThemeColors` through the function signatures (invasive refactor)
2. Use CSS custom properties like `var(--fm-text-color, #fallback)` (minimally invasive)

Option 2 is preferred because the theme CSS already defines these properties via `ThemeColors::to_css_vars()` in theme.rs, and SVGs with embedded `<style>` blocks will correctly resolve them.

## Specific Changes
- `render_layout_band()` line ~2095: Change `.fill("#4a5568")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #4a5568)")`
- `render_layout_axis_tick()` line ~2112: Change `.stroke("#94a3b8")` to `.stroke("var(--fm-edge-color, #94a3b8)")`
- `render_layout_axis_tick()` line ~2124: Change `.fill("#64748b")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #64748b)")`

## Status
PARTIALLY DONE — The CSS var approach was applied in the latest session. Verify the changes are committed and golden tests blessed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Band labels use `var(--fm-text-color)` with appropriate fallback
- Axis ticks use `var(--fm-edge-color)` for stroke and `var(--fm-text-color)` for text
- All 10 theme presets produce readable axis labels in gantt/timeline diagrams
- Golden SVG snapshots blessed after change","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:40.755243683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:00.042010891Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:00.041388102Z","close_reason":"Applied CSS var approach for band/axis colors in latest session","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
